metadata.title: China EOR Services | Employer of Record China – Jackson & Frank
metadata.description: China EOR and Employer of Record services. Hire employees in China without your own WFOE. Social insurance (五险), housing fund (公积金), PRC labour law, IIT payroll, and work permit support. Book a demo.
keywords: hire employees in China without entity

faqs.title: China EOR FAQs
faqs.subtitle: Quick answers on entity alternatives, social insurance, timelines, and compliance for Employer of Record in China.

items.question: How much does China EOR cost?
items.answer: China EOR pricing usually includes a monthly management fee per employee plus gross salary, employer social insurance and housing fund contributions (rates vary by city), and any statutory employer costs. Individual income tax (IIT) is withheld from employees. At Jackson & Frank we provide transparent, city-scoped quotes. Book a demo for numbers tailored to your hire location.

items.question: How fast can I hire someone in China with EOR?
items.answer: China onboarding typically takes longer than in some markets because employment registration, social insurance enrolment, and (for foreign nationals) work permit steps run in sequence. Many hires complete in roughly 2–6 weeks once contracts, credentials, and local documentation are in place. We set realistic milestones rather than overpromising.

items.question: Do I need a WFOE or local company to use China EOR?
items.answer: The point of China EOR is to employ staff through a compliant local employer structure so you are not immediately setting up your own WFOE or subsidiary. You direct the work; the EOR handles the employment contract, payroll, statutory contributions, and core HR compliance in line with PRC rules.

items.question: What about social insurance and the housing fund?
items.answer: Employers in China generally must participate in social insurance (五险) and the housing provident fund (公积金) where applicable, with contribution bases and caps set per city. We calculate and remit employer and employee portions according to local bureau rules and keep records for audits.

items.question: Can you support foreign nationals working in China?
items.answer: Foreign hires usually need an appropriate work permit and residence status aligned with the role and employer. We coordinate documentation with you and specialist partners where required. Eligibility and processing times depend on nationality, role, and local policy—ask us for a case-by-case assessment.
